title: Festuca peristerea
taxonomy: Festuca peristerea is classified as a species of grass within the family Poaceae. It is a member of the genus Festuca, commonly known as fescue grass. This genus comprises around 200-300 species of grasses, native to various parts of the world, including Europe, Asia, and North America. The exact classification of Festuca peristerea within the Festuca genus is not well-documented. More research is needed to determine its precise classification.
uses: Festuca peristerea is a species of fescue grass that has been traditionally used in various forms of medicine. Festuca peristerea is said to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may contribute to its potential medicinal uses. However, more research is needed to fully understand its effects and benefits. Traditionally, the plant has been used to treat various health conditions, including wounds, skin issues, and digestive problems. Further studies are necessary to confirm its efficacy and safety as a medicinal plant.

constituents: Festuca peristerea, also known as the mountain fescue, is a type of grass that contains various bioactive compounds. Festuca peristerea contains flavonoids, phenolic acids, and phenolic glycosides, which have been reported to possess antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities.
